title: libkrun: macOS resource limits cause periodic VM reboots (~every 60 min)
body:
### Issue Description

The podman machine VM reboots every ~60 minutes. macOS flags krunkit for exceeding disk write (2.1 GB in ~4h vs 2.1 GB/24h limit) and CPU wake limits (~550 wakes/sec). The VM receives a "Reboot key pressed short" via systemd-logind, triggering a clean reboot. `journalctl --list-boots` shows 20+ consecutive ~60 min sessions.

The heaviest stack in the macOS diag report is `BlockWorker::work -> pwritev` - normal VM root disk I/O, not a bug. Journald alone holds 2.6 GB. The problem is macOS treating krunkit as a regular background process subject to resource policing.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Start podman machine with libkrun provider
2. Run containers with moderate disk I/O (e.g. PostgreSQL + web app)
3. Wait ~60 minutes
4. VM reboots; `podman machine ssh -- journalctl -b -1 | grep "Reboot key"` shows Reboot key pressed short


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

VM journal (every boot ends the same way):
```
systemd-logind: Reboot key pressed short.
systemd-logind: Rebooting...
```
macOS diag report `(/Library/Logs/DiagnosticReports/krunkit_*.diag)`:
```
Event:        disk writes
Action taken: none
Writes:       2147.84 MB over 14584 seconds, exceeding limit of 24.86 KB/s over 86400 seconds
```
macOS system log:
```
process krunkit caught waking the CPU 45002 times over ~81 seconds, averaging 551 wakes/second
```

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

- macOS 26.5.1, Apple M1 Pro (MacBookPro18,1)
- Podman Desktop 1.28.2, podman 5.8.3
- krunkit 1.1.1, libkrun-efi
- VM: 5 CPUs, 3.7 GB RAM, 93 GB disk

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: @jakecorrenti @slp  Can you have a look at this?

> VM journal (every boot ends the same way):
> 
> ```
> systemd-logind: Reboot key pressed short.
> systemd-logind: Rebooting...
> ```

Could you please also paste a few lines before and after these messages?

From the guest perspective, this is an ordered shutdown, which makes me suspect those warnings from macOS are a red herring, since macOS is not capable of requesting libkrun to trigger the power button.
Also, is there any chance you're running some kind of security software (i.e. Crowdstrike) that does a local analysis on every listening TCP port?

@slp yes. CrowdStrike Falcon is running on the host machine. Here are the journal lines immediately before the reboot:
```
Jun 30 14:27:31.370995 sshd-session[115025]: banner exchange: Connection from 192.168.127.1 port 17520: invalid format
Jun 30 14:27:31.373226 db[1643]: invalid length of startup packet
Jun 30 14:27:31.386108 systemd-logind[1001]: Reboot key pressed short.
Jun 30 14:27:31.386133 systemd-logind[1001]: Rebooting...
Jun 30 14:27:31.386939 systemd-logind[1001]: System is rebooting.
Jun 30 14:27:31.396043 db[1643]: received fast shutdown request
```
`192.168.127.1` is the host-side gvproxy gateway. CrowdStrike runs as a macOS system extension intercepting network traffic at the stack level, so it doesn't appear in `lsof`/`netstat`, but the pattern is consistent - invalid SSH banner exchange and PostgreSQL startup packet from the host IP arrive ~15 ms before systemd-logind fires "Reboot key pressed short," repeating roughly every 60 minutes.

Could CrowdStrike's network discovery scan be triggering something in krunkit/libkrun's virtio-net or gvproxy path that results in the power button event?

I am experiencing similar issues, with the VM ejecting in less than a minute in some cases. I have worked through a number of debugging cases with the assistance of Cursor. 

Below is a summary of my current situation, hopefully it is helpful. Also attached are some logs that may be helpful.

# Podman Machine (libkrun) Recurring VM Deaths on macOS Tahoe 26.5.2

## Environment

- **macOS:** 26.5.2 (Build 25F84) — Tahoe
- **Hardware:** Mac14,10 (Mac Studio M2 Max), arm64, 32 GB RAM
- **Podman:** 6.0.0 (pkg installer from podman.io)
- **Fedora CoreOS:** 44.20260607.3.1, Kernel 7.0.11-200.fc44.aarch64
- **VM Provider:** libkrun (default for Apple Silicon)
- **CrowdStrike Falcon:** 7.34 (endpoint security agent — always running)

## Problem

The Podman machine VM (`krunkit` / libkrun provider) dies repeatedly after 20–60 minutes of uptime. The VM never survives longer than ~60 minutes. There are no crash reports, no macOS unified log entries, and no Jetsam events for `krunkit`. The process vanishes silently.

From inside the VM, `journalctl` shows a clean systemd shutdown sequence (`reboot.target` reached) with no internal trigger — no OOM-kill, no watchdog, no panic, no explicit `systemctl reboot` call. The shutdown is being initiated externally by the host.

The `applehv` provider (vfkit) was attempted as an alternative but fails to start with `VZErrorDomain Code=1 "Internal Virtualization error"`.

## Boot History (from `journalctl --list-boots` inside VM)

| Boot | Start | End | Duration |
|------|-------|-----|----------|
| -13 | Mon 18:06 | Mon 18:59 | **53 min** |
| -12 | Mon 22:22 | Mon 22:22 | **6 sec** (reboot loop) |
| -11 | Mon 22:23 | Mon 22:23 | **6 sec** (reboot loop) |
| -10 | Mon 22:23 | Mon 22:24 | **6 sec** (reboot loop) |
| -9 | Mon 22:24 | Mon 22:25 | **6 sec** (reboot loop) |
| -8 | Mon 22:26 | Mon 23:21 | **55 min** |
| -7 | Tue 08:53 | Tue 09:12 | **20 min** |
| -6 | Tue 09:14 | Tue 10:14 | **60 min** |
| -5 | Tue 10:35 | Tue 11:17 | **42 min** |
| -4 | Tue 11:24 | Tue 11:24 | **5 sec** |
| -3 | Tue 11:24 | Tue 11:40 | **16 min** |
| -2 | Tue 11:40 | Tue 12:19 | **39 min** |
| -1 | Tue 13:22 | Tue 13:23 | **50 sec** |
| 0 | Tue 13:24 | (running) | |

The VM never exceeds ~60 minutes of uptime.

## Root Cause Analysis

### 1. macOS Diagnostic Report for krunkit (disk writes)

A diagnostic report (`krunkit_2026-06-29-222715_jowood-mac.diag`) captured the following:

- **Event:** `disk writes`
- **Writes:** 8592.51 MB over 15651 seconds (549.02 KB/s average), **exceeding macOS limit of 99.42 KB/s over 86400s**
- **Memory footprint:** `1613.64 MB → 11.73 GB (+10.16 GB)` — krunkit's memory ballooned by **10 GB in ~1 minute**
- **Heaviest stack (691/691 samples):**
  ```
  BlockWorker::work → write_vectored_at_volatile → pwritev
  ```
  All samples are in libkrun's virtual block device I/O path (`libkrun.dylib`), stuck writing to the virtual disk via `pwritev`.

### 2. No External Kill Evidence

- **No crash reports** for krunkit on the day of the crashes
- **No macOS unified log entries** mentioning krunkit (even with `--info --debug`)
- **No Jetsam/memorystatus events** targeting krunkit
- **CrowdStrike `kill_process` count: 0** — Falcon is not directly killing it
- **No VM-internal trigger** — journalctl shows clean shutdown with no OOM, watchdog, or panic

### 3. AppleSystemPolicy Errors

During the crash window (12:19:25–12:19:33), repeated macOS kernel errors:
```
(AppleSystemPolicy) ASP: Unable to apply provenance sandbox: 268451845, /Applications/Podman Desktop.app/Contents/MacOS/Podman Desktop
```
Multiple `podman` processes also triggered DYLD shared region unnest warnings:
```
podman[PID] triggered unnest of range ... of DYLD shared region ... increases system memory footprint almost permanently
```

### 4. VM Internal Logs (journalctl -b -1)

The previous boot's journal shows only the shutdown sequence — no entries indicating what triggered the shutdown. The shutdown appears externally initiated:

```
Jun 30 13:23:13 systemd[1]: Reached target shutdown.target - System Shutdown.
Jun 30 13:23:13 systemd[1]: systemd-reboot.service: Deactivated successfully.
Jun 30 13:23:13 systemd[1]: Finished systemd-reboot.service - System Reboot.
Jun 30 13:23:13 systemd[1]: Reached target reboot.target - System Reboot.
Jun 30 13:23:13 systemd[1]: Shutting down.
```

No preceding OOM, watchdog, or explicit reboot command visible in the journal.

## Workload

The VM runs two podman-compose projects with ~20 containers total, including:
- 2× PostgreSQL instances (pgvector, Langfuse)
- ClickHouse (write-heavy analytics)
- SurrealDB (RocksDB-based)
- MinIO (object storage)
- Redis
- Multiple Python/Node.js application containers
- Crawl4AI (browser-based crawler with 1GB shm)

## Attempted Mitigations

| Mitigation | Result |
|---|---|
| Uninstalled Homebrew `libepoxy` (Library Validation conflict) | Fixed initial crash-on-start, but VM still dies after 20-60 min |
| Started machine from native terminal (not Cursor IDE) | Prevented process group kills, but VM still dies |
| Increased VM memory from 8 GB to 16 GB | No improvement |
| Added container logging limits (`max-size: 10m, max-file: 3`) | Not yet tested — VM died during database restore before compose restart |
| Added tmpfs mounts for ClickHouse/SurrealDB temp dirs | Not yet tested |
| Switched to `applehv` provider (vfkit) | `VZErrorDomain Code=1 "Internal Virtualization error"` — fails to start |

## applehv/vfkit Failure Details

```
Error: Error Domain=VZErrorDomain Code=1 Description="Internal Virtualization error. The virtual machine failed to start." UserInfo={
    NSLocalizedFailure = "Internal Virtualization error.";
    NSLocalizedFailureReason = "The virtual machine failed to start.";
}
```

- vfkit version: v0.6.1
- vfkit has the required `com.apple.security.virtualization` entitlement
- Podman auto-adds `--gui`, `--device virtio-gpu`, `--device virtio-input` flags — may be causing the failure
- No macOS Security & Privacy prompts blocking vfkit
- No log output from vfkit (log level set to "panic")

## Theory

libkrun's `BlockWorker` uses `pwritev` for virtual disk I/O. Under sustained container workloads, buffered writes accumulate faster than they can be flushed, causing krunkit's memory footprint to balloon (1.6 GB → 11.7 GB in ~1 minute). This triggers macOS resource enforcement or causes libkrun to fail internally. CrowdStrike Falcon's real-time scanning of every I/O operation amplifies the problem by slowing the flush rate.

## Questions for the Community

1. Is the libkrun `BlockWorker` memory growth (10 GB in 1 minute during sustained I/O) a known issue?
2. Is there a way to limit or throttle libkrun's block device buffering?
3. Why does `applehv`/vfkit fail with `VZErrorDomain Code=1` when the `--gui` flag is included? Is there a way to disable GUI mode in Podman 6.0?
4. Are there any macOS Tahoe-specific compatibility issues with Podman 6.0's libkrun provider?

[crowdstrike-impact.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29520015/crowdstrike-impact.txt)
[environment-info.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29520022/environment-info.txt)
[gvproxy.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29520018/gvproxy.log)
[podman-machine-default-boot.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29520020/podman-machine-default-boot.log)
[vfkit-applehv-failure.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29520019/vfkit-applehv-failure.txt)

Brief update of continued testing. I looked at the krunkit versioning and noticed that there were 2 releases since the version on my system. 

I used brew to upgrade to 1.3.0 ([https://github.com/libkrun/krunkit/releases#release-v1.3.0](https://github.com/libkrun/krunkit/releases#release-v1.3.0)) per the README.md and then ran the following commands to repoint Podman:

```shell
podman machine stop
sudo mv /opt/podman/bin/krunkit /opt/podman/bin/krunkit.1.2.1.bak
sudo ln -s /opt/homebrew/bin/krunkit /opt/podman/bin/krunkit
sudo mv /opt/podman/lib/libkrun.dylib /opt/podman/lib/libkrun.dylib.1.17.bak
podman machine start
```

Relaunched my containers and the database restore script. It survived the I/O processing and is still running. 

Fingers cross it holds past the hour threshold.

Did some additional testing, it looks like libkrun 1.17.0 may be a root issue. A build up of I/O was causing what appeared to be a memory leak. 

Ran some stress testing with the klibrun 3.0.0 / libkrun 1.19.0 combination (from the brew install) and things seem a lot more stable. 

Still running some test over night to see if it will break, but hopefully this helps you all resolve this.

> Did some additional testing, it looks like libkrun 1.17.0 may be a root issue. A build up of I/O was causing what appeared to be a memory leak.
> 
> Ran some stress testing with the klibrun 3.0.0 / libkrun 1.19.0 combination (from the brew install) and things seem a lot more stable.
> 
> Still running some test over night to see if it will break, but hopefully this helps you all resolve this.

@joewood-redhat macOS can't trigger a request to shut down the VM orderly, so this issue can't be related to resource consumption.
@slp should we also bump the KRUNKIT_VERSION in contrib/pkginstaller/Makefile to 1.3.1 to pick these changes up?
